title: Worth seeing death and rebirth
wikitext:
[]: Worth seeing death and rebirth?             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Oct 11, 2006 9:18 pm I've got the original 26 episodes, the directors cut versions of the last few episodes, and EoE. Is there anything new or different about death and rebirth that would merit its own viewing? To rephrase it, is there any new content or significant events that I'm missing out on by not watching death and rebirth?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Oct 11, 2006 9:30 pm Death draws some more connections, and has some supposed look-alikes of all the CHILDREN warming up for, and then playing, a string quartet. I usually skip over it when I'd just watched the series, or have just shown the whole series to someone. Skip Rebirth, it's just the first 30 min or so of EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 11, 2006 9:35 pm If you go by Death & Rebirth, odds are that you will be dissapointed. Rebirth isn't worth watching. Death however is quite good as a prelude to The End of Evangelion. If you don't have the time to watch the entire TV series befor hand, Death will get you in the right frame of mind to watch EoE and enjoyit to its fullest. Seeing as how Manga Entertainment is supposed to be putting the Renewal versions out at some point in the future, I would suggest waiting for that. Seeing as how there is no such thing as a Renewal version of Rebirth, I'm hoping that they'll do one release of Death and EoE in one package.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Oct 11, 2006 9:39 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: If you don't have the time to watch the entire TV series befor hand, Death will get you in the right frame of mind to watch EoE and enjoyit to its fullest.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Oct 11, 2006 11:07 pm There are also a few bits of animation that is unique only to Death. The only meaningful one I can think of is Rei I seeing Kaworu. More importantly (like cat42 mentioned) it isn't so much a recap of the first 24 episodes that makes it interesting, but HOW it recaps them. Events are stringed together in an interesting way, not chronological in story timeline nor series.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Thu Oct 12, 2006 5:43 am It's an awesome moodsetter, so I'll just reiterate what Monkey said; The Eva Monkey wrote: Death however is quite good as a prelude to The End of Evangelion. If you don't have the time to watch the entire TV series befor hand, Death will get you in the right frame of mind to watch EoE and enjoyit to its fullest.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Oct 12, 2006 11:20 am thanks for all the helpful responses. Also thanks for moving the thread (i think i put it in the discussion forum).

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Oct 12, 2006 3:07 pm I love Death; IMO it's the best part of Eva. Sure, it's mostly recycled footage, but it's put together beautifully. Skipping it just because you've already seen the series would be a horrible thing. But yeah, Rebirth is a waste of time.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 12, 2006 3:14 pm Space Penis wrote: I love Death; IMO it's the best part of Eva. Sure, it's mostly recycled footage, but it's put together beautifully.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Oct 12, 2006 3:34 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Space Penis wrote: I love Death; IMO it's the best part of Eva. Sure, it's mostly recycled footage, but it's put together beautifully.

[]:             Quote Post by ssetbob7 » Thu Oct 12, 2006 8:53 pm I've seen Death (online, youtube saves my life!) but can't seem to find Rebirth so I will take every body's word for it that I am not missing that much, what a relief.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 13, 2006 1:14 pm I know a site that has Rebirth on it. (*cough* Reichu-chan *cough* NGE Geeks Commentary page) Yes, Youtube is a very happy thing. If it weren't for that, I wouldn't have been able to see 15-EoE! (minus Rebirth) I personally think Death is brillantly edited and well-done, so what I don't understand is why are some some "farging ice-holes" out there that think it sucks? To quote my beloved Asuka: Anta baka? :?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Oct 13, 2006 2:20 pm It is a giant letdown if you've seen screenshots of new stuff and is expecting a movie. It's not a movie .

[]:             Quote Post by WES512 » Fri Oct 13, 2006 8:52 pm In Death, many of the TV scenes that were reworked turned out to be excellent, in special the one of Rei smiling at the end of episode 6. And there are scenes that make a connection to the events of EoE, for example this one: [URL=http://img301.imageshack.us/my.php?image=snapshot20061009001716hh4.jpg] [/URL] Because of these two points, I recommend to watch Death.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Oct 14, 2006 2:55 pm youtube? oh come on.. isohunt and torrentspy all the way. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sat Oct 14, 2006 3:22 pm slothen wrote: youtube? oh come on.. isohunt and torrentspy all the way. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Oct 16, 2006 1:50 am cat42 wrote: QFPT

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Oct 16, 2006 2:04 am Quoted for pirate truth :P

Is there anything new or different about death and rebirth that would merit its own viewing? To rephrase it, is there any new content or significant events that I'm missing out on by not watching death and rebirth?
